label: Network Architecture Diagram Upload
upload_label: Upload your SWIFT secure zone network architecture diagram. Accepted formats: PDF, PNG, Visio (.vsdx), draw.io, PowerPoint, SVG. The diagram must show: zone boundaries, firewalls, all SWIFT components, back-office paths, and operator PC classification. Diagram must be dated within the last 12 months.
guide: Upload a dated network diagram (PDF/Visio/PNG) showing the SWIFT secure zone boundary, all in-scope components, firewalls, and data-flow arrows.
Question-Level AWS Sources: VPC, Subnets, Route Tables, IGW, NAT GW, TGW — ec2:DescribeVpcs + DescribeSubnets + DescribeRouteTables
Reason / Rationale: AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can auto-generate the underlying configuration snapshot. However, a formatted, dated, human-reviewed document/file is still required for formal submission. AWS evidence supplements and validates the upload.

label: Diagram version / date (must be within last 12 months)
placeholder: YYYY-MM-DD
guide: Enter the version date of the diagram — it must fall within the last 12 months.
Reason / Rationale: Review, approval, and policy dates are institutional records that must be confirmed by a responsible human owner.

label: Is there any direct internet path from any system inside the secure zone?
options: ["No","Yes","Unknown"]
guide: Confirm whether any system inside the secure zone has a path to the internet — 'No' is the expected compliant answer.
Question-Level AWS Sources: VPC Route Tables, IGW, Security Groups, NACLs — ec2:DescribeRouteTables, DescribeInternetGateways, DescribeSecurityGroups, DescribeNetworkAcls
Reason / Rationale: AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can automatically retrieve this configuration state. APIs: ec2:DescribeVpcs, DescribeSubnets, DescribeRouteTables, DescribeInternetGateways, DescribeNatGateways, DescribeTransitGateways.

label: If YES — provide business/technical justification and compensating controls
placeholder: e.g. No direct internet path exists; proxy used with whitelist and IDS monitoring.
guide: Required only if answer above is Yes or Unknown — state the business reason, compensating controls, and relevant firewall rule references.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Describe how the secure zone boundary is drawn and protected
placeholder: e.g. Secure zone is isolated via dedicated firewall cluster between DMZ and core LAN.
guide: Describe the logical/physical method used to isolate the secure zone (e.g., dedicated VLAN, separate switch, firewall policy).
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Confirm all SWIFT components are visible inside the zone
options: ["All components visible and labelled","Most visible — some unlabelled","Some components outside zone with justification","Not all components visible"]
guide: Confirm every in-scope SWIFT component (messaging interface, GUI, HSM, connectors, jump server, operator PCs) is labelled on the diagram.
Question-Level AWS Sources: VPC, EC2 Instances, RDS, ELB — ec2:DescribeInstances (SWIFT-role tag filter), ssm:DescribeInstanceInformation
Reason / Rationale: AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can automatically retrieve this configuration state. APIs: ec2:DescribeVpcs, DescribeSubnets, DescribeRouteTables, DescribeInternetGateways, DescribeNatGateways, DescribeTransitGateways.

label: Are bridging servers identified with network placement on the diagram? (v2026 mandatory)
options: ["Yes","No — not applicable","No — needs to be added"]
guide: Confirm whether bridging/middleware servers are drawn and positioned on the diagram — mandatory for v2026.
Question-Level AWS Sources: EC2 Instances (tag: swift-role=bridging), VPC Subnet placement — ec2:DescribeInstances, DescribeSubnets
Reason / Rationale: AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can automatically retrieve this configuration state. APIs: ec2:DescribeVpcs, DescribeSubnets, DescribeRouteTables, DescribeInternetGateways, DescribeNatGateways, DescribeTransitGateways.

label: Are customer client connectors shown and labelled? (v2026 mandatory for A1/A4)
options: ["Yes","No — not applicable","No — needs to be added"]
guide: Confirm whether customer client connectors are labelled on the diagram — mandatory for A1/A4 architectures in v2026.
Question-Level AWS Sources: EC2 Instances (tag: swift-role=customer-connector), VPC topology — ec2:DescribeInstances, DescribeVpcs
Reason / Rationale: AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can automatically retrieve this configuration state. APIs: ec2:DescribeVpcs, DescribeSubnets, DescribeRouteTables, DescribeInternetGateways, DescribeNatGateways, DescribeTransitGateways.

label: Protocol/encryption annotations on data flows — clarifications
placeholder: e.g. [Enter your response here — one or two sentences describing the relevant detail].
guide: List each inter-component flow with the protocol used (e.g., LAU+TLS, two-way TLS, TLS 1.2+) and flag any unencrypted flows.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Back-office connectivity summary (v2026 — now mandatory under Control 2.4)
placeholder: e.g. All items reviewed and compliant; 0 exceptions identified in latest assessment.
guide: Summarise which back-office systems connect to the secure zone, through what path (direct or via bridging server), and the encryption used.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Customer connector zone boundary description (A1 architecture only)
placeholder: e.g. Control implemented via dedicated hardware with restricted access and audit logging.
guide: For A1 only — describe the customer connector zone boundary, its firewall protection, and how it achieves equivalent security to the main zone.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Known documentation gaps and remediation plan
placeholder: e.g. No gaps identified; all controls meet CSCF v2026 requirements.
guide: List any incomplete labels, missing flows, or components not yet documented, and state the planned remediation date.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (Amazon VPC, EC2, AWS Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: SWIFT Component Inventory Upload
upload_label: Upload your SWIFT component inventory (spreadsheet preferred). Must include ALL in-scope systems: hostname, IP, OS version, role, zone membership (Secure Zone / Customer Zone / DMZ / Enterprise), production/test/DR status, and physical vs virtual. Use the built-in inventory table below or upload your own spreadsheet.
guide: Upload a dated network diagram (PDF/Visio/PNG) showing the SWIFT secure zone boundary, all in-scope components, firewalls, and data-flow arrows.

label: Systems excluded from the secure zone — with justification
placeholder: e.g. Justified due to operational requirement; compensating controls applied and documented.
guide: For any system intentionally excluded from the secure zone, provide the hostname and a CSCF-aligned business/technical justification.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Co-hosting decisions — non-SWIFT systems co-located in the zone
placeholder: e.g. [Enter your response here — one or two sentences describing the relevant detail].
guide: List any non-SWIFT systems co-hosted in the secure zone and confirm they receive equivalent security controls.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Bridging server details (v2026 mandatory)
placeholder: e.g. BRDG-01 acts as relay between Alliance Access and ERP; TLS 1.3, port 8443.
guide: For each bridging/middleware server, provide hostname, role, back-office systems connected, and the security controls applied.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Customer connector and client connector details (v2026 mandatory for A1/A4)
placeholder: e.g. Alliance Connect Gold (server-side); handles payment initiation, TLS mutual auth.
guide: List all customer connectors and client connectors with type, hostname, version, and architecture classification.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Customer connectivity zone details (A1/A4 architecture only)
placeholder: e.g. Component: Server-01; role: messaging interface; location: secure zone rack A3.
guide: For A1/A4 — describe the customer connectivity zone components (Alliance Connect VPN instances, dedicated PCs, jump server).
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Known inventory gaps and remediation plan
placeholder: e.g. No gaps identified; all controls meet CSCF v2026 requirements.
guide: Document any inventory discrepancies, pending additions, or components not yet fully captured, with expected completion dates.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (EC2, SSM, Config) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Data Flow Diagram Upload
upload_label: Upload your SWIFT data flow diagrams. Must show ALL flows between SWIFT components with: source, destination, direction arrows, protocol annotation (e.g. LAU+TLS, two-way TLS), and encryption method. Separate diagrams for internal flows and back-office flows are acceptable. Include cross-environment flows (on-prem to cloud) if applicable.
guide: Upload a dated network diagram (PDF/Visio/PNG) showing the SWIFT secure zone boundary, all in-scope components, firewalls, and data-flow arrows.
Question-Level AWS Sources: VPC Flow Logs, TGW Flow Logs, VPC Peering, VPN Tunnels — ec2:DescribeFlowLogs, DescribeVpcPeeringConnections, DescribeVpnConnections
Reason / Rationale: AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can auto-generate the underlying configuration snapshot. However, a formatted, dated, human-reviewed document/file is still required for formal submission. AWS evidence supplements and validates the upload.

label: Internal data flow inventory — clarifications not visible on diagram
placeholder: e.g. Item 1: [name, version, role]; Item 2: [name, version, role].
guide: For each flow not fully visible on the diagram, provide source, destination, direction, protocol, and encryption method.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Back-office data flow classification (v2026 mandatory — Control 2.4)
placeholder: e.g. [Enter your response here — one or two sentences describing the relevant detail].
guide: Classify each back-office flow as: new direct (mandatory protection), bridging server flow (mandatory protection), or legacy direct (advisory).
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Encryption method summary per flow type
placeholder: e.g. TLS 1.3 with AES-256-GCM for all internal flows; certificates renewed annually.
guide: State the TLS version and cipher suite for each flow category — confirm compliance with SWIFT KB 5021566.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Legacy flow migration plan (if any unprotected legacy flows exist)
placeholder: e.g. Legacy FTP flow to be replaced with SFTP by 2026-06-30; risk-accepted until then.
guide: For any unprotected legacy direct flows, provide a migration plan with target date (advisory until 2028, then mandatory).
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: HSM connection flow details
placeholder: e.g. HSM01 connected via dedicated serial link to Alliance Access on isolated VLAN.
guide: Describe the HSM connection flows not shown on the diagram: source, destination, protocol, and encryption method.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Cross-environment flow details (on-premises to cloud)
placeholder: e.g. Component: Server-01; role: messaging interface; location: secure zone rack A3.
guide: Describe any flows crossing on-premises and cloud boundaries — confirm encryption method at each boundary crossing point.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

guide: Describe backup replication, data extract, or offline processing paths from the secure zone to external destinations.
Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

Reason / Rationale: While AWS (VPC Flow Logs, CloudWatch, TGW) can provide supporting configuration data, a human must write the explanatory narrative, justification, or contextual description required by this field.

label: Architecture Declaration Form Upload
upload_label: Upload your formal SWIFT architecture type declaration document. This can be a signed declaration form, a letter, or a formal document. It must state the declared architecture type (A1/A2/A3/A4/B), the BIC(s) in scope, and the rationale. If no separate document exists, complete all fields below.
guide: Upload a dated network diagram (PDF/Visio/PNG) showing the SWIFT secure zone boundary, all in-scope components, firewalls, and data-flow arrows.
Reason / Rationale: Architecture type declaration (A1/A2/A3/A4) is a human business decision. AWS APIs cannot determine which CSCF architecture type applies to an institution. The uploaded document must be provided manually.

label: Declared architecture type
guide: Select the CSCF architecture type (A1–A4 or B) that best matches your SWIFT deployment — choose the most comprehensive if in doubt.
Reason / Rationale: Architecture type declaration (A1/A2/A3/A4) is a human business decision. AWS APIs cannot determine which CSCF architecture type applies to an institution. No AWS API can answer this question; human input required.

label: Transaction Business Control Procedure Document Upload
upload_label: Upload your transaction business controls procedure document.
